title: Angles, lenses, lighting basics (cinematography 101 → pro practice)

house_style.zh: 默认带角度：少纯平视；固定机位仅在极必要时

eye_level.use: Rare default in this project — only when neutrality is the story point

slight_low.use: Default body/portrait lift; quiet power without comic hero low

slight_high.use: Default for vulnerability, observation, table/desk inserts

dutch_mild.use: Unease without full canted chaos; good for suspicion/dread

dutch_angle.use: Psychological imbalance; motivated by dread/disorientation/oppression peaks

ots.use: Conversation, relational pressure — combine with slight high/low

angle_by_subject.person: use emotion table; never default pure eye_level

focal_length_mm.18_24: Ultra wide: space distortion, environment dominance, unease if overused on faces
focal_length_mm.28_35: Wide-standard: body in space, documentary-cinematic walk-and-talk
focal_length_mm.65_85: Portrait isolation, romantic or intense singles, shallow depth
focal_length_mm.100_plus: Spy/tele compression, compressed backgrounds, surveillance feel

lighting_ratios.motivated_light: Prefer sources that could exist in story world (window, practical lamp, neon)
lighting_ratios.key_fill_back: Key shapes face; fill controls contrast; optional edge separates subject
lighting_ratios.high_key: Bright, low contrast — comedy, openness, safety
lighting_ratios.low_key: Dark, high contrast — drama, noir, secrecy, dread
lighting_ratios.contrast_as_emotion: Raise contrast on pressure/reveal; soften on intimacy/grief soft
lighting_ratios.protect_eyes: Even in low key, keep eyes readable unless story hides them

composition_basics: Rule of thirds and intentional center-punch for power
Headroom and lead room for gaze/motion direction
Negative space can express loneliness or dread
Angle + height together: do not write eye-level + chest height as a fake angle
